question: Create a simple menu-driven program (e.g., 'A' for Add, 'S' for Subtract, 'Q' for Quit) that continuously prompts the user for choices until 'Q' is entered. Use a `do-while` loop and a `switch` statement.

test_cases.expected:
Menu:
A - Add
S - Subtract
Q - Quit
Enter your choice (A/S/Q): Enter two numbers to add: Result: 8

Menu:
A - Add
S - Subtract
Q - Quit
Enter your choice (A/S/Q): Enter two numbers to subtract: Result: 8

Menu:
A - Add
S - Subtract
Q - Quit
Enter your choice (A/S/Q): Exiting program. Goodbye!

Menu:
A - Add
S - Subtract
Q - Quit
Enter your choice (A/S/Q): Invalid choice. Please try again.

Menu:
A - Add
S - Subtract
Q - Quit
Enter your choice (A/S/Q): Enter two numbers to add: Result: 2

Menu:
A - Add
S - Subtract
Q - Quit
Enter your choice (A/S/Q): Exiting program. Goodbye!

driver: This program will not compile due to a syntax error. A driver would typically compile and then execute the program, redirecting stdin and capturing stdout. Since compilation will fail, execution cannot proceed.
errors: Missing semicolon after std::cout statement on line 13 (before std::cin >> choice;), preventing compilation.
